repository: Lewis University Library AI Vibe Code Repository
description: Example project dataset using the canonical AI Vibe Code Repository metadata model. Categories are application types; projects are interactive, browser-based tools built through human expertise and AI-assisted code generation.

projects.title: Faculty AI Evaluation Toolkit
projects.description: A decision-support application that helps faculty evaluate classroom, research, and administrative AI use cases by aligning direct contextual needs with tool affordances, risk considerations, and teaching goals.
projects.author: Jake Bailey
projects.purpose: Support context-sensitive AI tool selection for teaching, learning, research, and university work.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with interface scaffolding, use-case synthesis, rule organization, and iterative code revisions; library expertise shaped criteria, language, citations, and review logic.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for usability, citation logic, link integrity, accessibility, and alignment with faculty support needs.
projects.accessibility_notes: Includes keyboard-accessible controls, responsive typography, and visible focus states.

projects.title: Clinical Scenario Builder
projects.description: An interactive nursing simulation generator that helps instructors create patient scenarios, assessment prompts, and debrief questions for skills lab or classroom discussion without using real patient data.
projects.author: Dr. Maria Santos
projects.purpose: Give instructors a reusable way to design safe, fictional practice scenarios for clinical reasoning.
projects.ai_role: AI generated interface drafts and scenario template variations; faculty reviewed clinical accuracy and removed protected-data risks.
projects.human_review: Checked for fictionalized patient details, nursing relevance, and classroom usability.
projects.accessibility_notes: Plain-language prompts, high-contrast controls, and print-friendly scenario output.

projects.title: Aviation Weather Pattern Explorer
projects.description: A visual training tool that lets aviation students explore how weather variables shape flight planning decisions through interactive diagrams, scenario cards, and reflective checks.
projects.author: Prof. Daniel Reyes
projects.purpose: Help students connect meteorological concepts to aviation decision-making.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with diagram logic, scenario copy, and responsive layout; aviation expertise shaped safety framing and instructional sequence.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for conceptual accuracy and non-operational educational framing.
projects.accessibility_notes: Text alternatives for visual states and keyboard navigation for scenario panels.

projects.title: Citation Pathway Coach
projects.description: A library instruction tool that guides students through choosing when and how to cite sources, paraphrase responsibly, disclose AI assistance, and document research decisions.
projects.author: Lewis University Library Instruction Team
projects.purpose: Support research transparency and citation decision-making across courses.
projects.ai_role: AI helped draft branching prompts and interface states; librarians edited for instruction tone, policy alignment, and citation clarity.
projects.human_review: Reviewed against library instruction outcomes and public-facing academic integrity language.
projects.accessibility_notes: Uses semantic buttons, clear labels, and short decision steps.

projects.title: Chemistry Lab Safety Scenario Studio
projects.description: An interactive lab-prep application where students identify hazards, select safety actions, and receive feedback before entering a wet-lab environment.
projects.author: Dr. Aisha Patel
projects.purpose: Improve safety readiness through practice before students encounter lab materials.
projects.ai_role: AI produced early hazard card structures and feedback language; faculty corrected safety content and simplified interactions.
projects.human_review: Checked by chemistry faculty for educational appropriateness and safety accuracy.
projects.accessibility_notes: Hazards are represented with text labels in addition to icons.

projects.title: Literature Review Mapper
projects.description: A humanities and social sciences research tool that helps students organize article summaries into themes, tensions, methods, and evidence clusters for literature review planning.
projects.author: Dr. Lena Hartwell
projects.purpose: Support synthesis-oriented research habits without automating the student's argument.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with sorting interface prototypes and explanatory language; faculty revised the workflow to preserve student authorship and interpretation.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for writing pedagogy, student agency, and accessibility of research prompts.
projects.accessibility_notes: Cards can be moved through buttons rather than drag-and-drop alone.

projects.title: Assignment AI Disclosure Builder
projects.description: A quick generator that helps faculty create assignment-specific AI-use statements, disclosure expectations, and student-facing guidance aligned with local course goals.
projects.author: Center for Teaching & Learning Excellence
projects.purpose: Help instructors translate abstract AI guidance into clear assignment-level language.
projects.ai_role: AI generated wording variations and interface scaffolding; teaching-and-learning staff refined tone, boundaries, and course-level adaptability.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for clarity, flexibility, and alignment with institutional teaching support.
projects.accessibility_notes: Generated text appears in editable text areas with copy buttons and screen-reader labels.

projects.title: Confidence Interval Studio
projects.description: A statistics learning application that lets students adjust sample size, confidence level, and variability to see how intervals change and why interpretation matters.
projects.author: Dr. Minh Tran
projects.purpose: Make statistical estimation visible and interactive for introductory learners.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with JavaScript calculation logic, UI variants, and explanatory text; faculty verified formulas and examples.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for calculation accuracy and instructional clarity.
projects.accessibility_notes: Results are announced in an aria-live region and visual changes include text equivalents.

projects.title: Cybersecurity Incident Response Game
projects.description: A branching, team-based game where students triage alerts, communicate decisions, and reflect on technical and ethical dimensions of cybersecurity incidents.
projects.author: Prof. Noah Williams
projects.purpose: Practice response decision-making in a low-risk, discussion-rich simulation environment.
projects.ai_role: AI drafted branching story paths and UI components; faculty revised for disciplinary accuracy and non-sensitive scenarios.
projects.human_review: Reviewed to avoid operational exploit guidance and to foreground ethical reasoning.
projects.accessibility_notes: Playable with keyboard and includes non-timed mode.

projects.title: Ethical Case Debate Simulator
projects.description: A discussion tool that presents layered ethical scenarios, assigns stakeholder perspectives, and helps students prepare evidence-based positions before class debate.
projects.author: Dr. Sophia Nguyen
projects.purpose: Prepare students to analyze ethical tensions through multiple perspectives before discussion.
projects.ai_role: AI helped generate neutral scenario scaffolds; faculty revised cases for nuance, fairness, and classroom fit.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for balanced representation of stakeholder positions and sensitivity of examples.
projects.accessibility_notes: Includes printable prompts and clear heading structure.

projects.title: First-Generation Resource Navigator
projects.description: A student-support application that helps first-generation students identify campus services, prepare questions, and map next steps based on common academic and belonging-related needs.
projects.author: Student Success Team
projects.purpose: Reduce navigation friction by connecting common student questions to campus resources and next-step language.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with flow design and approachable language; student-success staff reviewed resources and tone.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for resource accuracy, non-clinical framing, and student-centered language.
projects.accessibility_notes: Uses large tap targets, plain-language steps, and no required login.

projects.title: Course AI Policy Builder
projects.description: A faculty-development tool that helps instructors create course-specific AI-use policies by selecting allowed practices, disclosure expectations, rationale, and assessment considerations.
projects.author: Dr. Priya Raman and Lewis University Library
projects.purpose: Help faculty make transparent, assignment-aware AI expectations visible to students.
projects.ai_role: AI generated draft policy language and conditional logic; faculty and librarians refined options for clarity and instructional nuance.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for student-facing clarity and institutional fit.
projects.accessibility_notes: Output is copyable, editable, and organized with headings.

projects.title: Library Instruction Request Planner
projects.description: A workflow tool that helps faculty prepare a library instruction request by identifying course context, research skills, assignment timing, and desired student outcomes.
projects.author: Lewis University Library Instruction Team
projects.purpose: Make library instruction requests more context-rich and easier to translate into active learning sessions.
projects.ai_role: AI assisted with intake flow and plain-language prompts; librarians refined instructional outcomes and routing language.
projects.human_review: Reviewed against library instruction workflow and service language.
projects.accessibility_notes: Form steps include labels, hints, and live summary output.

projects.title: Alt Text Practice Studio
projects.description: An accessibility learning application that lets students and faculty practice writing alt text for different academic image types, compare versions, and reflect on context-specific description choices.
projects.author: Office of Accessibility and Lewis University Library
projects.purpose: Support inclusive teaching and digital accessibility through practice, comparison, and reflection.
projects.ai_role: AI helped generate practice image contexts and feedback patterns; accessibility staff reviewed guidance and examples.
projects.human_review: Reviewed for accessibility pedagogy and context-sensitive description guidance.
projects.accessibility_notes: Tool content itself models accessible image-description practice and keyboard flow.
